sanskrit:
अयुक्तः प्राकृतः स्तब्धः शठो नैष्कृतिको लसः।
विषदी दीर्घसूत्री च कर्ता तामस उच्यते॥
transliteration:
ayuktaḥ prākṛtaḥ stabdhaḥ śaṭho naiṣkṛtiko lasaḥ |
viṣadī dīrghasūtrī ca kartā tāmasa ucyate ||
literal_meaning: A doer who is undisciplined, vulgar, arrogant, deceitful, malicious, lazy, despondent, and procrastinating is declared to be in the mode of darkness.
contextual_meaning: Krishna paints the psychological profile of a person whose actions are driven by the tamasic guna: energy turned inward, heavy, and self-sabotaging. Such a person ignores ethical scripts, operates from raw impulse or inertia, and converts potential into chronic dissatisfaction.
psychological_state: Learned helplessness fused with covert hostility; the person is stuck in a cycle of avoidance, self-loathing, and external blame, producing life-draining procrastination and depression.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media for hours while deadlines loom, feeling numb yet vaguely angry, promising "tomorrow" while self-esteem erodes.
anchor_text: Rise above inertia and self-sabotage; act with clarity and purpose.
